mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises due to seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, which compounds into sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to generate positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing weakness and supply-side bottlenecks, forcing equity markets to price in lower earnings and generating negative returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 65% of contraction episodes, or the signal is completely overridden by concurrent monetary easing.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above 3.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 4.0%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 3.0% and FEDFUNDS stays below 4.0%
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation/low-rate regime produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate consumption and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates capital flight into real estate at the expense of consumer goods, weakening broad equity earnings and producing negative monthly returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 5% growth while RSAFS declines
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of divergence episodes, or the housing-retail split shows no predictive power after adjusting for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing consumer price inflation signals improving supply chain efficiency and margin expansion, which directly boosts corporate profitability and drives positive weekly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM crosses above CPIAUCSL MoM
failure_condition: The outperformance condition yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the mechanism breaks down when global commodity prices spike independently of domestic production.
